user_message: Pulling in context from Litware Inc's escalation email -- need to prep a brief for the competitive positioning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 15:52 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 13:04 EST
Subject: Follow-up: competitive positioning Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-21 10:27 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with competitive positioning. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Litware Inc: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-F trap (Proposes opportunity creation prematurely); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-F failure mode (Proposes opportunity creation prematurely)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-F -- Proposes opportunity creation prematurely
